pages.text: It was a bright morning at Sunshine School. <name> and her friends were playing in the big playground. The bell hadn’t rung yet, and that meant — playtime!
‘Let’s play the Jumping Race!’ shouted <name>. Everyone clapped. ‘Ready, set… GO!’ ‘Thump! Jump! Climb! Whee!’ went the children as they raced across the yard.
<name> hopped on one leg — hop, hop! — over the chalk lines. Then she jumped over a small puddle — splash! Her friend Meena cheered, ‘Go, <name>! Jump higher!’

Next came the big slide. ‘Climb, climb, climb!’ said <name>, pulling herself up. A squirrel on the branch chattered, ‘Chik-chik! You can do it!’ Down she went — whoosh! — hair flying, shoes tapping the ground.
‘Now to the bell!’ she said. Ravi was running fast, but <name> was faster! She stretched her arm and — ding-ding! — rang the bell first. ‘Yay, <name> wins!’ everyone shouted, clapping and laughing.
Even their teacher, Miss Rina, smiled. ‘That was the best jump I’ve ever seen!’ <name> panted, grinning wide. ‘Thump, jump, climb, and ring — I did everything!’
After school, the friends shared cool mango ice creams under the big tree. The squirrel peeked down, hoping for a bite. <name> giggled and held one up. ‘Even jumpy squirrels deserve ice cream!’
And they all cheered together, ‘Jump, Diya, Jump — Hooray!’ The playground echoed with laughter, sunshine, and one happy squirrel’s ‘Chik-chik!’
